metadata.title: Killer Instinct
metadata.arcade_board: Midway Wolf Unit (R4600 MIPS CPU, custom hardware)
metadata.tagline: The arcade fighting game that changed combos forever.

arcade.notes: Hard drive-based arcade board, first of its kind

snes.notes: Bundled with Killer Cuts music CD. Rare feat of SNES hardware.

game_boy.notes: Surprisingly full roster despite hardware limits

development.technology: Pre-rendered 3D character sprites created in SGI workstations, then converted to 2D sprite sheets. Same technique as Donkey Kong Country.
development.fun_fact: Killer Instinct was originally a tech demo to showcase what would become the Nintendo Ultra 64 (N64) hardware. The arcade version ran on a hard drive — unheard of at the time.
innovations: First arcade cabinet to use a hard drive instead of ROM chips
Pre-rendered 3D sprites at a time when most fighters were hand-drawn
Combo system that tracked and announced hit counts in real-time
Double health bar system with No Mercy finishers

platforms.name: Arcade
platforms.hardware: Midway Wolf Unit — R4600 MIPS 100MHz, 8MB RAM, custom DMA, hard drive storage
platforms.notes: Definitive version. Full animation frames, CD-quality announcer, cabinet with large speakers.

platforms.name: SNES
platforms.notes: Reduced animation frames and audio samples. Still impressive for SNES. Bundled with Killer Cuts CD.

platforms.name: Game Boy
platforms.notes: Full roster present. Simplified combos and visuals. Remarkable portable conversion.

gameplay.objective: Defeat your opponent by depleting both of their health bars across best-of-three rounds.
gameplay.round_structure: Each fighter has TWO health bars. Deplete the first to start round 2. When the second bar flashes red, Ultra Combos become available.
gameplay.signature_system: Combo chaining — Opener → Auto-Double → Linker → Auto-Double → Ender. Each piece can be light, medium, or fierce strength.
unique_mechanics: Combo Breakers — Interrupt opponent's combo by matching the strength of their auto-double (light breaks light, medium breaks medium, fierce breaks fierce)
Ultra Combos — Massive cinematic combos available when opponent's 2nd health bar is flashing. Can exceed 20+ hits.
No Mercy / Danger Moves — Finishing moves performed at the end of the final round. Stage-specific environmental kills.
Humiliation — Forces opponent to dance uncontrollably. The ultimate disrespect.
Ultra Ender — End an Ultra Combo early for guaranteed damage instead of risking a drop
Double health bar system — Each round has two full health bars, making matches longer and more dramatic
Knockdown recovery — Different wakeup timings create okizeme (wake-up pressure) situations
gameplay.combo_system_explained: Combos in KI are not freestyle — they follow a grammar. An Opener starts the combo. Auto-Doubles are automatic two-hit attacks that follow. Linkers are special moves that connect auto-doubles together. Enders finish the combo with bonus damage or knockdown. The defender can break auto-doubles by matching strength, creating a mind game within the combo itself.

buttons.quick_punch: QP — Light punch. Fast, low damage. Hard to break.
buttons.medium_punch: MP — Medium punch. Balanced speed and damage.
buttons.fierce_punch: FP — Heavy punch. Slow but powerful. Easiest to react to.
buttons.fierce_kick: FK — Heavy kick. Slow, high damage.

movement.walk_backward: Hold joystick away from opponent (also blocks standing attacks)
movement.jump: Tap up. Can jump forward, backward, or neutral.
movement.crouch: Hold down. Crouch blocks low attacks.

universal_mechanics.throw: Close range — Forward + FP or FK
universal_mechanics.combo_breaker: During opponent's auto-double — match the strength with your own attack button (QP breaks QP, MP breaks MP, FP breaks FP, same for kicks)

combo_structure.opener: A special move or jump-in attack that starts a combo. Examples: Jago's Wind Kick, Sabrewulf's Jumping Slash.
combo_structure.auto_double: After an opener or linker, pressing any attack button triggers an automatic 2-hit attack. Strength determines speed and damage. Light = fast but weak, Fierce = slow but strong and breakable.
combo_structure.linker: A special move performed during a combo to connect auto-doubles. Extends the combo. Each character has unique linkers.
combo_structure.ender: A special move that ends the combo. Different enders have different properties — damage ender, launcher ender, battery ender.
combo_structure.structure: Opener → Auto-Double → Linker → Auto-Double → Linker → Auto-Double → Ender

defense.blocking: Hold back to block high/mid attacks. Hold down-back to block low attacks. No chip damage on normals.
defense.combo_breaker: The signature defensive mechanic. During an opponent's auto-double, input the SAME strength attack to break the combo. Requires reads — guess wrong and nothing happens. Guess right and the combo ends immediately.
defense.spacing: Keep distance using long-range normals and projectiles. Many characters have tools to control space.
defense.anti_air: Use crouching fierce or character-specific anti-air specials to stop jump-ins.

systems.meter: No traditional super meter. Ultra Combos are gated by opponent's health (2nd bar flashing). Combo Breakers have no cooldown — pure read-based.

characters.name: Jago
characters.lore: A Tibetan monk chosen by the Tiger Spirit to fight in the tournament. The 'Ryu' of KI.
characters.playstyle: All-around. Good at every range. Strong fireball and uppercut. Best character to learn the game with.

signature_moves.name: Wind Kick
signature_moves.description: Rushing kick. Great opener and linker.

signature_moves.name: Tiger Fury
signature_moves.description: Rising uppercut. Anti-air and combo ender.

signature_moves.name: Laser Blade
signature_moves.description: Spinning blade attack. Good combo linker.

characters.ultra_combo: Wind Kick opener into extended auto-double chain — 18+ hits
characters.tip: Start here. Endokuken to control space, Wind Kick to get in, Tiger Fury to anti-air. Simple but effective.

characters.name: B. Orchid
characters.lore: A secret agent investigating Ultratech. Jago's sister (revealed later in the series).
characters.playstyle: Aggressive rushdown with fast mixups. Ichi Ni San gives frame advantage. Flik Flak crosses up.
characters.ultra_combo: Rapid Ichi Ni San chains — 20+ hits
characters.no_mercy: Flash attack (infamous — the most controversial No Mercy in the game)
characters.tip: Pressure relentlessly. Mix Ichi Ni San with throws. Don't give opponents room to breathe.

characters.name: Glacius
characters.lore: An alien being made of ice, captured by Ultratech for the tournament.
characters.playstyle: Keep-away and zoning. Long-range normals, ice lance, and cold shoulder to control space. Weak up close.
characters.ultra_combo: Ice Lance into extended freeze combos — 17+ hits
characters.tip: Stay at full screen. Zone with Ice Lance. Use Cold Shoulder to punish jumps. Avoid close range.

characters.name: Thunder
characters.lore: A Nez Perce warrior searching for his lost brother Eagle.
characters.playstyle: Grappler with command throws and raw power. Slower but devastating damage per hit.
characters.ultra_combo: Command grab into slam chain — 15+ hits
characters.tip: Get in close. Tick throws with light attacks into Call of Earth. One good grab can turn the match.

characters.name: Sabrewulf
characters.lore: Count Von Sabrewulf, a nobleman cursed with lycanthropy. Enters the tournament seeking a cure.
characters.playstyle: Pure aggression. Fastest character in the game. Relentless pressure with Ragged Edge and Jumping Slash.
characters.ultra_combo: Ragged Edge loops — 22+ hits. One of the longest Ultras.
characters.tip: Never stop attacking. Sabrewulf's speed lets him overwhelm slower characters. Mix Ragged Edge with throws.

characters.name: Spinal
characters.lore: An ancient warrior resurrected by Ultratech. A living skeleton fueled by dark magic.
characters.playstyle: Tricky and unpredictable. Teleports, shield absorb, and curse mechanics. High skill ceiling.

signature_moves.name: Soul Sword
signature_moves.description: Skull projectile that curses on hit.

characters.ultra_combo: Teleport mixup into curse chain — 19+ hits
characters.tip: Play mind games. Teleport to confuse, absorb projectiles with Shield, and curse opponents to limit their options.

characters.name: Fulgore
characters.lore: Ultratech's ultimate weapon. A cybernetic soldier prototype designed to win the tournament.
characters.playstyle: Balanced with strong zoning and decent rushdown. Eye Laser for range, Cyber Dash for approach. Jack of all trades.
characters.ultra_combo: Eye Laser into Cyber Dash chain — 20+ hits
characters.tip: Zone with Eye Laser, punish approaches with Plasma Storm, and rush down when you have the life lead.

characters.name: Cinder
characters.lore: Ben Ferris, a convicted criminal transformed into a being of living flame by Ultratech experiments.
characters.playstyle: Aerial dominance. Juggles and air combos. Inferno controls ground, aerial attacks control air.
characters.tip: Control the air. Use Inferno to force opponents to jump, then catch them with Trailblazer. Flashy and fun.

characters.name: Riptor
characters.lore: A genetically engineered raptor with cybernetic enhancements. Ultratech's biological weapon.
characters.playstyle: Low-hitting rushdown. Fast, dangerous close-range mixups. Tail flip and claw rake keep opponents guessing.
characters.ultra_combo: Claw Rake into Tail Flip chain — 17+ hits
characters.tip: Stay low and fast. Mix Claw Rake with Tail Flip overhead. Riptor's low attacks are some of the best in the game.

characters.name: TJ Combo
characters.lore: A disgraced heavyweight boxing champion with cybernetic arm implants. Fights to reclaim his title.
characters.playstyle: Pure rushdown boxing. Fastest combo potential in the game. Tremor for range, Powerline for approach.
characters.ultra_combo: Powerline into Rolling Thunder — 24+ hits. Highest hit count Ultra in the game.
characters.tip: Get in and stay in. TJ's auto-doubles are fast and hard to break. Pressure with Powerline and throw when they freeze.

characters.name: Eyedol
characters.lore: An ancient two-headed demon lord. The final boss of the tournament.
characters.playstyle: Overpowered boss character. Massive damage, long reach, teleport stomp. Not balanced for competitive play.

signature_moves.name: Stomp
signature_moves.description: Ground stomp that hits anywhere on screen.

characters.tip: The boss. Banned in most competitive settings. If you can select him (secret code), enjoy the chaos. Stomp is absurdly good.

versions.differences: Original release. Some infinite combos possible.
Reduced animation frames. Simplified stages. Fewer voice samples. Added training mode. Bundled with Killer Cuts CD.
Monochrome. Simplified combos. All 11 characters present. Impressive for the hardware.

beginner: Start with Jago. His moveset is straightforward and teaches KI fundamentals.
Learn ONE opener for your character. Wind Kick (Jago) or Ragged Edge (Sabrewulf) are good starters.
Practice short combos first: Opener → Auto-Double → Ender. Resist the urge to extend.
Block! Hold back to block high, down-back to block low. Many beginners never block.
Learn to combo break. Listen for the auto-double sound — if it's slow, it's fierce. If it's fast, it's quick.
intermediate: Extend combos: Opener → Auto-Double → Linker → Auto-Double → Ender. This doubles your damage.
Mix auto-double strengths to make combo breaking harder. Alternate light and fierce.
Learn your character's anti-air. Stopping jump-ins is crucial in KI.
Use throws as a mixup tool. If the opponent is blocking too much, throw them.
Study your opponent's patterns. Do they always break fierce? Then use light auto-doubles.
advanced: Bait combo breakers by intentionally pausing in your combo. If they mash break, punish.
Learn Ultra Combo timing. Start the Ultra when the 2nd health bar is flashing — too early and it won't activate.
Master Opener → Manuals (manual auto-doubles with precise timing) for unbreakable combo extensions.
Use stage positioning. Corner carry with combos gives you better okizeme (wake-up pressure).
Study matchup-specific strategies. Each character pairing has different optimal ranges and punishes.

cheats.name: Combo Breaker
cheats.effect: Interrupt opponent's combo by matching auto-double strength. The most important defensive tool.

cheats.name: Select Eyedol
cheats.effect: At character select, perform a specific button code to unlock the boss character.

cheats.name: Random Select
cheats.effect: Hold up and press start at character select for a random fighter.

cheats.name: Ultra Combo Activation
cheats.effect: When opponent's 2nd health bar flashes red, perform your Ultra input during a combo for a devastating finisher.

cheats.name: Humiliation
cheats.effect: End-of-match finisher that forces your opponent to dance. Requires specific input after winning the final round.

trivia: Killer Instinct was the FIRST arcade game to use a hard drive for storage instead of ROM chips.
The 'C-C-C-COMBO BREAKER!' announcer call became one of the most iconic sound bites in all of gaming.
Ken Lobb, the game's design lead, has a gun named after him in GoldenEye 007 — the Klobb.
The SNES version shipped with the 'Killer Cuts' music CD — a rare pack-in that featured arranged versions of every stage theme.
Rare used Silicon Graphics Inc. (SGI) workstations to create the pre-rendered 3D character models — the same technology used for Jurassic Park's CGI.
KI was originally conceived as a tech demo to showcase the upcoming Nintendo Ultra 64 (later renamed N64).
The announcer's voice was performed by Chris Sutherland, a Rare programmer who also voiced characters in Banjo-Kazooie.
TJ Combo's name is a play on the real boxer Thomas 'The Hitman' Hearns.
Eyedol was intentionally overpowered as the final boss. Players developed specific anti-Eyedol strategies involving spacing and patience.
The attract mode on the arcade cabinet was designed to be as loud and flashy as possible — arcade operators loved it because it drew crowds.
KI's combo counter would track hits up to 80+ — some extended combos lasted over 15 seconds.
The game's announcer had over 50 unique voice callouts including 'MONSTER COMBO', 'AWESOME COMBO', 'BLASTER COMBO', and 'KING COMBO'.

arcade_cabinet.controls: 8-way joystick + 6 attack buttons per player (QP, MP, FP, QK, MK, FK)
arcade_cabinet.layout: Two-player side-by-side configuration. Japanese-style ball-top joystick.
arcade_cabinet.audio: DCS stereo sound system with large dedicated speakers mounted in the cabinet
features: Large top-mounted speakers for booming announcer audio
Attract mode with full combo showcases and character intros
Coin-op with adjustable difficulty settings for operators
Cabinet art featuring all main characters in action poses
arcade_cabinet.notable: The loud announcer and speaker system made KI cabinets the noisiest machines in any arcade. You could hear 'ULTRA COMBO!' from across the room.

cultural_impact.influence_on_genre: KI's combo system influenced virtually every fighting game that followed. The concept of structured combo routes (opener/linker/ender) became standard. The combo breaker mechanic introduced meaningful defensive counterplay during combos — something Street Fighter didn't have.
cultural_impact.legacy: Spawned Killer Instinct 2 (arcade, 1996), Killer Instinct Gold (N64, 1996), and a critically acclaimed Xbox One reboot (2013) by Double Helix / Iron Galaxy. The franchise has remained relevant for 30 years.
cultural_impact.in_popular_culture: 'Combo Breaker' became internet slang used far beyond gaming. The phrase appears in memes, sports commentary, and everyday conversation to mean 'interrupting a streak.' FGC (Fighting Game Community) major tournament 'Combo Breaker' is named after this mechanic.
cultural_impact.soundtrack: Killer Cuts (the pack-in SNES CD) is considered one of the best fighting game soundtracks. Tracks like 'The Way U Move' (Orchid) and 'K.I. Feeling' (main theme) have a cult following.

guide_generation.voice: Write like you're a veteran arcade player who was THERE in 1994, telling stories to the next generation. Mix facts with atmosphere. Make the reader feel the quarters on the cabinet, the crowd behind them, the announcer shaking the speakers.
